channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump gives mixed signals on Iran war. And, how Epstein built ties to scientists
item.description: President Trump provided conflicting messages about when the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will end. And, NPR investigates how late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein leveraged ties with scientists.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump provided conflicting messages about when the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran will end. And, NPR investigates how late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein leveraged ties with scientists.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: 'Pro-worker AI,' streaming fatalities, and other fascinating new economic studies
item.description: From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.
item.content:encoded:
From artificial intelligence to fatalities from music streaming to the effects of immigrants on elderly health care, the Planet Money newsletter rounds up some interesting new economic studies.

(Image credit: KIRILL KUDRYAVTSEV)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: GLP-1s have transformed weight loss and diabetes. Is addiction next?
item.description: A large study found that people taking GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic for diabetes were less likely to be diagnosed with substance use disorder.
item.content:encoded:
A large study found that people taking GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic for diabetes were less likely to be diagnosed with substance use disorder.

(Image credit: Maria Fabrizio for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Trump gives mixed messages about when the war with Iran will end
item.description: In a phone call with CBS News Monday, Trump said "the war is very complete." But at a separate event with Republican lawmakers, he said the U.S. still needed to achieve "ultimate victory."
item.content:encoded:
In a phone call with CBS News Monday, Trump said "the war is very complete." But at a separate event with Republican lawmakers, he said the U.S. still needed to achieve "ultimate victory."

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump hails Iran successes but offers no end date, Lebanon wants talks with Israel, and two teens are charged in NYC attack attempt.
item.content:encoded: Trump hails Iran successes but offers no end date, Lebanon wants talks with Israel, and two teens are charged in NYC attack attempt.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Out of work and with 2 teens, this mom may lose food stamps under Trump's changes
item.description: Policy experts say new SNAP changes don't address the challenges faced by single parents. They also argue that losing food assistance will only create more barriers for struggling families.
item.content:encoded:
Policy experts say new SNAP changes don't address the challenges faced by single parents. They also argue that losing food assistance will only create more barriers for struggling families.

(Image credit: Caroline Yang for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Why Congress rarely pushes back when presidents deploy military force
item.description: The Constitution gives Congress the power to declare war, but presidents assert broad authority over use of force and the military. Congress has done little to push back.
item.content:encoded:
The Constitution gives Congress the power to declare war, but presidents assert broad authority over use of force and the military. Congress has done little to push back.

(Image credit: The White House)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: One year later: Mahmoud Khalil remains in limbo but ready to fight
item.description: The case of Khalil, who was detained last March, sits at the vanguard of a battle of immigrants' due process and civil rights, and the Trump administration's mass detention and deportation policies.
item.content:encoded:
The case of Khalil, who was detained last March, sits at the vanguard of a battle of immigrants' due process and civil rights, and the Trump administration's mass detention and deportation policies.

(Image credit: Stephanie Keith)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Georgia special election to replace MTG tests the power of Trump's endorsement
item.description: Voting ends Tuesday night in the district that former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene left this year after a feud with President Trump. It's unclear if his pick will win her spot.
item.content:encoded:
Voting ends Tuesday night in the district that former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene left this year after a feud with President Trump. It's unclear if his pick will win her spot.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kallis

item.title: Why the 'mad scramble' to fill hormone therapy prescriptions for menopause
item.description: With the removal of FDA warning labels, hormone therapy to treat symptoms of menopause has grown in popularity. Now some patients are reporting delays in filling prescriptions for estrogen patches.
item.content:encoded:
With the removal of FDA warning labels, hormone therapy to treat symptoms of menopause has grown in popularity. Now some patients are reporting delays in filling prescriptions for estrogen patches.

(Image credit: SVPhilon)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey
